extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection via Jinja2. User-controlled 'name' query parameter (line 19) is interpolated into an f-string on line 38 to build 'content', which is then passed to Template(content).render() on line 41. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., ?name={{7*6}} or ?name={{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}) to achieve Remote Code Execution.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (app/config.py line 11) with no URL validation or allowlist. requests.get(config.PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL) is called without validating the URL destination. If an attacker can influence this environment variable, they can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially accessing internal services or sensitive data.

Hardcoded credentials in source code. Lines 13-15 contain hardcoded sensitive values (SUPER_SECRET_NAME and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN) that should be loaded from environment variables instead. Although marked with FIXME comments indicating intent to remediate, these credentials are currently exposed in the codebase and git history.
